experiment: ADR-098 nucleus: real surface gates real-LLM solve of a real test (ADR-109)
method: A real buggy module (merge-intervals, touching-interval bug) + a real Node test, placed among 40 same-overlap distractor files. A variant's REAL contextBuilder (buildContext, run via node --experimental-strip-types) selects which files the LLM sees. If the buggy file survives into its window, the LLM (gemini-2.5-flash) is given it + the failing test and asked to fix it; the REAL test is the verdict.

results.note: contextBuilder window too narrow → buggy file not in context → LLM never sees it (0 calls)

conclusion: The harness's REAL contextBuilder surface GATES whether a REAL LLM can fix a REAL failing test: narrow window hides the bug (unsolvable), wide window surfaces it (solved, real test passes). All three layers real — surface code + LLM + test command — for <$0.0005. This is the self-improvement premise demonstrated end-to-end: evolving the surface changes what the agent can solve.
honest_scope: Single bug, single-file fix, hand-built repo — the ADR-098 nucleus, not a SWE-bench run. Scaling to real multi-file corpora is the remaining build.
